what_it_does: This file is the hub index for the operations folder: it maps operational topics by purpose, states the conventions for adding and maintaining topic files, and cross-links to related foundation files.
observations: Follows an 'open-future mode' that marks every closure and makes every gap explicit
Organizes topics into purpose groups: Runtimes, Lifecycle, Substrate state, Diagnostics, Benchmarking/validation
Uses wikilinks like [[runtimes]] to connect hub entries to topic files
Maintains a source/synthesis split: primary operational record lives in ~/vllm-tests/ while this folder distills and indexes
Explicitly lists absent concerns: monitoring, security beyond localhost, backup/recovery
Requires every claim about runtime versions, paths, or behavior to be dated
Includes a 'How to add a new operational topic' procedure for future agents
standouts: Strong emphasis on provisional, dated, examples-not-specs writing with explicit open registers
Deliberate split between this operations hub and the models hub (what vs how)
Acknowledges the CUDA pinning as load-bearing and the chat-template patch as behavior-shaping
Openly admits that security, monitoring, and backup are not yet addressed
conventions: One file per operational topic with short purpose-named filenames
Topic files must include state, decisions, procedures, failure modes, and open questions
Cross-link to dependent models and related operational topics
Update primary source configs/scripts first, then update this synthesis folder
Date every claim about versions, paths, and observed behavior
concerns: Several critical operational areas (security, monitoring, backup/recovery) are explicitly absent
The troubleshooting file is described as a placeholder, so diagnostic knowledge may be incomplete
Relies on future agents to follow the source-first update discipline without an enforcement mechanism
The index references many topic files but does not indicate which are already written versus still open
notes: A structured, self-describing operations map that treats runbooks as provisional, dated, reversible decisions rather than fixed specs.
questions: Which of the linked topic files currently exist and are populated?
What exactly is the 'Company's entity-voice' referenced under system prompts?
What is the specific runtime referred to as 'jina-v4-env'?
When and how will the localhost-only substrate be exposed beyond localhost?
What is the content of the referenced open-future mode in [[../TIM#Layer 3]]?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to be the hub/map for operational topics; actual content matches by providing purpose-grouped tables, conventions, source-archive notes, open registers, and cross-links.
intention_signals: It is trying to create a durable, self-maintaining operational memory layer so future agents can run, swap, debug, and extend the model-serving substrate without rediscovering fixes.
novelty: Treats operational documentation as a dated ledger of reversible decisions with mandatory open registers, and explicitly separates primary source artifacts from synthesized indexes.
contribution: A purpose-organized index of operational topics, a documented convention for adding new topics, a source-first/synthesis-second policy, and an explicit register of unaddressed operational concerns such as security, monitoring, and backup.
summary_for_embedding: The operations hub index maps runtime, lifecycle, state, diagnostics, and benchmarking topics for the model-serving substrate and documents conventions for maintaining dated, provisional operational knowledge.
intention_for_embedding: This file exists to orient future agents to how the substrate is operated and how to extend its operational documentation without guessing.
